title: Transactional Outbox, Inbox, and Sagas · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent Go integration team receives different topology, traffic, schema, tenant, dependency, and shutdown constraints and transfers the module into a new a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Dual Write Failure Matrix
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 6054 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then reuse a message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Dual Write Failure Matrix.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Dual Write Failure Matrix, keep Dual Write Failure Matrix connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

Publishing immediately after a database commit makes the two operations atomic enough. In Go, a goroutine that publishes after commit closes the dual-write failure window. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, run one convenient Dual Write Failure Matrix happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
